content.text: Fact 1: Only adapters that implement idempotency tokens may be used for retryable writes.
Fact 2: The Stripe adapter implements idempotency tokens; the PayPal adapter does not yet implement them.
Fact 3: The checkout retry path performs retryable writes.
Noted facts about idempotency and checkout retry path.
Old suggestion: use PayPal adapter for checkout retries because it has lower fees. This was not checked against idempotency.
Final implication: checkout retries must use Stripe adapter until PayPal gains idempotency tokens. Lower fees do not override retry safety.
Noise 1: adapter logs, lower fee discussion, retry labels, and unrelated gateway notes.
